senses.definition: A plural form referring to a small genus of shrubs in the Penaeaceae family, native to southern Africa.
examples: Sarcocollas are studied for their unique flower structure.
Researchers collected samples of several Sarcocollas species.

senses.definition: A chemical component or protein associated with certain biological tissues (usage may be obscure or specialized).
examples: The assay used sarcocollin as a marker.
Researchers identified sarcocollin in the tissue sample.

senses.definition: A large intramuscular cyst of a protozoan parasite from the genus Sarcocystis.
examples: Infected meat contained visible sarcocysts.
Veterinarians examined sarcocysts in muscle tissue.

senses.definition: A taxonomic category or group related to sarcocysts, often used in parasitology to describe related organisms or life-cycle stages.
examples: The Sarcocystidea include several genera of cyst-forming parasites.

senses.definition: Relating to sarcocystidean parasites or their cyst-forming life stages; describing organisms in this group.
examples: Sarcocystidean parasites exhibit a two-host life cycle.
Researchers studied sarcocystidean cyst walls.

senses.definition: An organism or element belonging to the group Sarcocystidia or a closely related taxonomic unit (usage is specialized).
examples: The specimen matched a sarcocystidian profile.

senses.definition: A genus of parasitic protozoa that form sarcocysts in muscle tissue of various hosts.
examples: Sarcocystis species can infect livestock and wildlife.
Sarcocystis has a complex life cycle involving two hosts.

senses.definition: Third-person singular present of sarcocystise, meaning to form or develop sarcocysts.
examples: Infected tissue sarcocystises over time.

senses.definition: A sarcocyst-like body or structure resembling a sarcocyst, used in parasitology.
examples: The drawing showed a sarcocystoid structure in muscle tissue.

senses.definition: A cell forming part of a sarcocyst or a small sarcomere-like unit in muscular tissue.
examples: The sarcocyte was observed under the microscope.

senses.definition: The soft living substance within a cell; cytoplasm or internal cell content in some contexts.
examples: Researchers studied the sarcode of the protozoan cell.

senses.definition: The outer layer of a sarco-, relating to a muscular cell or tissue (rare, specialized terminology).
examples: Microscopic sections highlighted the sarcoderm.

senses.definition: A term referring to flesh-colored tissue or muscle tissue in a biological context; used in parasitology or histology.
examples: The sample contained sarcoderma around muscular fibers.

senses.definition: Plural of sarcode; basic cytoplasmic content of cells.
examples: Cell sarcodes were observed in stained slides.

senses.definition: Relating to sarcodes or sarcodic tissue; pertaining to protozoan or cellular content.
examples: Sarcodic material was analyzed under electron microscopy.

senses.definition: A genus or taxonomic group within certain protozoan organisms; used in specialist biology.
examples: Sarcodictyum spp. were described in parasitology records.

senses.definition: A subphylum or broad grouping of amoeboid protozoa that move and feed by pseudopodia.
examples: Sarcodina includes many amoeboid organisms.

senses.definition: Pertaining to or resembling sarcode or sarcodous tissue.
examples: Sarcodous material was found in the sample.

senses.definition: A benign bone tumor (enchondroma) associated with sarcoidosis-like changes in some texts; used in medical morphology.
examples: Radiographs showed a sarcoenchondroma in the patient’s rib.

senses.definition: Plural of sarcoenchondroma; multiple benign bone tumors with sarcoid-like changes.
examples: The patient had two sarcoenchondromas removed.

senses.definition: Plural of sarcoenchondroma; alternate plural form used in medical literature.
examples: Case reports described several sarcoenchondromata.

senses.definition: Capable of producing sarcoma or related tissue changes; used in pathology.
examples: Certain viruses are sarcogenic in animals.

senses.definition: Causing sarcoma or sarcoma-like growths; relating to sarcomagenesis.
examples: Sarcogenic agents were implicated in the study.

senses.definition: Producing flesh or muscle tissue; related to sarcoma formation.
examples: The tumor showed sarcogenous features.

senses.definition: An anatomical term referring to a protective or supportive tissue in certain organisms; used by specialists.
examples: Sarcoglia layers were studied in the sample.

senses.definition: A genus name (taxonomic) for certain birds of prey; used in zoological contexts.
examples: Sarcogyps species are studied by ornithologists.
